v2.title: The awakening census under the provenance signature
v2.subtitle: Coding chronological stratum and attribution over the canonical awakening events re-splits the canon: the 'canonical' awakening corpus is overwhelmingly a LATE-canonical, hagiographic-narrated stratum, not the early Buddha-word it reads as on the structural axis alone.
v2.framework: PROVENANCE-SIGNATURE.md (chronological stratum I.1, attribution I.2, recall ladder 3.1)

triage.question_type: Typology, category (the members are asserted events, so epistemic/attribution is load-bearing per the framework's typology-category row)

excluded_axes.II.7 epistemic marking: the census enumerates whether an awakening is narrated, not whether it is staked under a verification formula; the load is on WHEN and BY WHOSE VOICE, which stratum + attribution carry
excluded_axes.I.4 cross-recensional: coded only link-level as a secondary corroboration of the early floor; the corpus is Pali-only and the apadana genre's lateness is settled philologically, not by the parallel table
excluded_axes.I.5 pre-Buddhist: the awakening event is a Buddhist datum by definition; inheritance is not in question
excluded_axes.I.8 harmonization: no doctrinal tension is being reconciled; the census is descriptive
excluded_axes.I.6 reception / I.7 edition: fired automatically: the niggahita-glyph recall cliff is an edition/segmentation fact, reported in the recall ladder; no load-bearing English term carries a claim

v2.headline: Of the 299 structurally-mula (canonical) awakening events, only 38 are early-canonical; 261 carry the mula tag while coding late-canonical, paracanonical, or commentary-era on the chronological axis. The single largest class, the Apadana (162 events), re-codes late-canonical. On attribution the corpus is overwhelmingly narrated about the awakened (284 of 299 redactor-narrated or self-reported), but NOT, as an earlier pass wrongly coded it, with zero Buddha-vacana: 15 of the 299 rows (7 distinct, deduplicated recollections) are the Buddha asserting his OWN awakening in the first person, and the Buddha also declares others' attainments. The canonical awakening corpus is late and mostly narrated, yet the Buddha is both the first-person asserter of his own bodhi and the authoritative declarer of others'.

stratigraphy.note: Chronological stratum coded INDEPENDENTLY of the mula/attha/tika structural layer, from work + position (PROVENANCE-SIGNATURE I.1). A row whose layer is mula but whose stratum is not early-canonical is the analytically interesting disagree case.

warrants.buddhavamsa: Buddhavamsa, a late-canonical KN stratum (the lineage of past Buddhas)
warrants.niddesa: Maha-/Cula-niddesa, a canonical exegesis of the Sutta-nipata, late-canonical
warrants.nikaya-prose: AN/SN/MN/DN sutta prose, the consensus early-canonical floor
warrants.udana: Udana, an older mixed prose-and-verse KN collection
warrants.apadana: Apadana, a recognised late-canonical KN stratum (autobiographical merit-and-awakening verse)
warrants.thera-therigatha: Thera/Therigatha verse: the gathas carry archaic material, but the awakening narration in these rows sits in the redactor-compiled biographical frame; coded contested-confidence (register-relative, not a secured date)
warrants.vinaya-nidana: Vinaya frame-narrative (nidana) position; late by occasioning-story position, a position label rather than a secured date, coded contested-confidence
warrants.visuddhimagga: Visuddhimagga (Buddhaghosa), structurally tagged mula in this corpus but classical-commentary on stratum: layer and stratum disagree
warrants.vimanavatthu: Vimanavatthu, a late-canonical KN verse collection

attribution.note: Per CLAIM (per row, not per work-class), the illocutionary owner of the attainment-claim (I.2). The honest default for a flat narrated awakening with no quotative owner is a redactor-frame code, not buddha-vacana; the buddha-vacana category is coded per row, recall-anchored by the bodhisatta-frame / paccaññāsiṃ discriminator and confirmed by k=3 blind coders (IAA kappa=1.0). An earlier pass coded attribution by work-class and wrongly hard-zeroed this category.
attribution.iaa: k=3 independent blind coders, fixed 6-value codebook, judged from Pali excerpts only. kappa=1.0 is scoped to the 15 retained BV attribution rows, not the 9-bucket precipitating-condition coding. Of the original 17 BV candidate rows, 15 are 3/3 unanimous buddha-vacana on the catalogued attainment-claim; the 2 large work-level rows cst-s0304m.mul-sn4_1 (a 311,658-char SN volume row) and cst-vin02m2.mul-vin3_1 (a 243,393-char Vinaya volume row) are RECODED redactor-frame: the kappa packet excerpted the whole monolithic work-level row, not the catalogued span, and the catalogued attainment-event in each is a third party (Rahula, Yasa) narrated flatly, with the Buddha's first-person self-claim sitting elsewhere in the same large row. Boundary rows: sn8.12 + sn52.24 unanimous named-disciple-self-report; sn47.29 + sn47.30 unanimous named-disciple-dialogue; sn55.39 buddha-declares-another (2/3, 1 indeterminate). Rows coded indeterminate by all three (dn5, mn74, mn147, sn35.121) were packet-excerpt-truncation artifacts: read in full they are redactor-frame (dhammacakkhu / mind-freed narrated flatly).
attribution.relational_test: Named search for the Buddha declaring ANOTHER's attainment, mula: 29 rows carry the destiny-declaration formula (byakareyya + sotapanno); 10 carry 'annam byakasi'; the Nadika mirror-of-Dhamma post-mortem declarations (opapatiko..parinibbayi with bhagava) span DN/MN/SN/AN. The claim 'the Buddha asserts only his own awakening, never another's' is REFUTED: the Buddha both asserts his own bodhi (first person) and authoritatively declares others' attainments (byakarana).
attribution.recall_observation: The census under-enumerated the Buddha's own-awakening corpus. The bodhisatta-before-awakening first-person frame appears in 48 mula rows corpus-wide (incl. mn26 Ariyapariyesana, mn14, mn128, an3.103, an5.68, an5.196, an8.64, an9.41, sn12.4/10/65, sn14.31, sn22.26, sn35.13/14/117, sn36.24, sn51.11/21, ps2.2), most outside the 299 census universe. The within-299 figure (15 rows / 7 recollections) is therefore a floor bounded by the original census's recall, not the canon's full count. Reported as a measured recall observation, not a re-census (regression gate: the 299 stays fixed).

quotative_scan.note: of the 299 mula events, 206 carry first-person self-narration markers (the Apadāna / Theragāthā 'I attained' verse); only 22 carry bhagavā etadavoca; 48 carry the redactor's evaṃ me sutaṃ / tena samayena frame

recall_ladder.strategy: naive surface string 'arahattaṁ pāpuṇi' (bar-niggahita glyph, as a non-specialist types it), ILIKE
recall_ladder.note: the CST corpus writes niggahita as the dotted-m glyph (ṃ), so the bar-m (ṁ) surface string finds almost nothing

recall_ladder.strategy: glyph-normalized + declension/sandhi (dotted-m ṃ; pāpuṇi/patto/pattā/āpādi), regex over the arahatta-attainment family
recall_ladder.note: the glyph normalization is the dominant recovery; +912 over rung 1

recall_ladder.strategy: concept/periphrasis union, name-independent (desanāpariyosāne, gāthāpariyosāne, anupādāya āsavehi cittaṃ vimucci, virajaṃ vītamalaṃ dhammacakkhuṃ udapādi, khīṇā jāti, aññaṃ byākāsi) across all markers
recall_ladder.note: the mass-awakening formula desanāpariyosāne is 0 in mula and 206 in attha, a commentarial narrative marker; +574 over rung 2

recall_ladder.strategy: false-positive purge (doctrinal/abstract uses of the markers set aside as non-events by the close read)
recall_ladder.note: the census reports 4,023 classified marker passages, of which 2,214 narrate a real event and 1,809 are doctrinal/abstract and were set aside; the 2,214 is the net event count after the purge

early_nikaya_prose_external.note: of the 33 early-canonical Nikāya-prose awakening events, external (non-Pāli) parallels exist for several, a link-level proxy for a pre-sectarian core of the bare awakening narration

apadana_buddhavamsa_external.note: link-level only; the Apadāna/Buddhavaṃsa works carry some external parallels at work level, but the genre is philologically late-canonical; container-vs-feature kept distinct, coded link-level

regression.note: The recall ladder did not move the 2,214 event count: the census was already built at the dotted-m glyph depth (rung 2+), so the rung-1 cliff is a Methods finding about the surface string, not a recall correction to the census. The GROUP BY work_role reconfirms the dataset's layer counts exactly.

v2.verdict: CONFIRMED and SHARPENED on stratigraphy; CORRECTED on attribution. The original headline (awakenings live mostly in the commentaries, not the canon) holds and deepens: even the 'canon' share is mostly a LATE-canonical, hagiographic-narrated stratum, and the canon/commentary contrast is, in part, an early -> late gradient INSIDE the canon. The attribution sub-claim is corrected: the earlier 'zero Buddha-vacana, never staked by the Buddha as his own claim' was a per-work-class coding artifact. Coded per row, 15 of the 299 (7 distinct recollections) ARE the Buddha asserting his own awakening in the first person, and the Buddha also declares others' attainments. The awakening corpus is mostly narrated about the awakened, but the Buddha is both its first-person self-asserter and the authoritative declarer of others'.

prediction_score.prediction: the commentarial/Apadana/Jataka awakening events are a *late* stratum (chronology), not merely a separate voice; and that some are redactor-narrated, not Buddha-vacana. PASS if >=1 large event-class re-codes late-canonical.
prediction_score.evidence: the Apadana, the single largest mula class (162 events, >= the 30-event large-class threshold), re-codes late-canonical; 261 of 299 mula rows carry a layer/stratum disagreement; and 284/299 mula events are non-Buddha-vacana (redactor-frame or hagiographic self-report). On attribution the per-claim re-code corrects the earlier per-work-class zero: 15 rows are buddha-vacana (7 distinct recollections).
